name: Crayfish Tails a La Carlton
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
1. In a large skillet, bring 1 quart water, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon sugar, parsley leaves, and 12 stalks fresh dill to a boil. Simmer for 10 minutes.
2. Add the frozen crayfish tails (2 lbs) to the skillet. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes. Remove the crayfish from the liquid and discard the intestinal vein.
3. In a small saucepan, whisk together 1 cup hollandaise sauce, 1 tablespoon finely chopped dill, 1/2 teaspoon sugar until warm. Gradually whisk in 1/3 cup dry white wine until combined. Be careful not to let the sauce get too hot. Pour the sauce over the crayfish and serve with cooked white rice.
